data.title: Higher Oil Prices Keep Gold On Track For Monthly Decline
data.description:
Gold prices rose on dip-buying interest but remained on track for a second consecutive monthly decline as elevated oil prices and expectations of prolonged higher interest rates weighed on sentiment.

Spot gold climbed 1% to US$4,588.09 per ounce as of 0736 GMT after hitting its lowest level since 31 March in the previous session. Despite the rebound, bullion was still down about 1.7% for the month so far.

U.S. gold futures for June delivery rose 0.4% to US$4,578.10.

“Gold has struggled again this month as oil strength has dominated the narrative. Rising crude pushes up inflation expectations and interest rate forecasts, which in turn caps gold’s appeal,” said Tim Waterer, chief market analyst at KCM Trade.

However, he added that support is emerging from bargain hunting and expectations that geopolitical tensions may eventually ease.

“However, a combination of bargain-hunting and expectations that a peaceful resolution to the (U.S.-Iran) conflict will be found at some point are providing something of a floor for gold,” he said.

The move in gold came as Brent crude oil rose above US$124 a barrel after reports that the United States was considering potential military action against Iran, fuelling concerns over further disruptions to already constrained Middle East supply.

Monetary policy developments also weighed on sentiment. The U.S. Federal Reserve left interest rates unchanged on Wednesday but signalled increased concern over inflation, in a divided decision that saw three dissenting votes.

Markets are now pricing in no rate cuts this year, with expectations of a 30% chance of a rate hike by March 2027, sharply higher from about 5% previously, according to FedWatch data.

While gold is traditionally viewed as an inflation hedge, higher interest rates tend to reduce its appeal as a non-yielding asset.

Other precious metals also rose in line with gold. Silver gained 1.8% to US$72.78 per ounce, platinum rose 2.9% to US$1,934.54 and palladium increased 1.9% to US$1,486.82. All three metals were also on track for a second monthly decline.

Reuters

data.title: Stock Picks: KSL And Lim Seong Hai Capital
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified KSL Holdings and Lim Seong Hai Capital as potential trading ideas, citing improving technical momentum and bullish price action.

RHB Research said KSL Holdings is attempting to extend its counter-trend rebound after climbing above its 21-day simple moving average line. The stock recently bounced off the RM3 psychological support level on strong trading volume, signalling easing selling pressure.

The research house noted that a break above the RM3.15 resistance could see KSL resume its upward movement towards RM3.30, with further upside to RM3.45. However, it cautioned that a fall below RM3 would indicate a bearish breakout and could trigger renewed downside pressure.

Meanwhile, RHB Research said Lim Seong Hai Capital may continue its upward trajectory after breaking past the RM1.85 resistance on strong volume. The stock has printed two consecutive bullish candlesticks, suggesting strengthening momentum.

It added that the counter could extend gains towards RM2, followed by RM2.15 if the positive trend holds. On the downside, a drop below the RM1.70 support level would negate the bullish outlook.

data.title: Selling Pressure Sweeps Across Market As More Counters End In Red Territory
data.description:
The stock market movement ended in the red territory on April 30 as broad-based selling pressure outweighed selective gains, with losers decisively outnumbering gainers across the market.

At 5pm, some 816 counters ended in the red compared to 360 gainers, while 546 stocks were unchanged, reflecting cautious investor sentiment.

Total turnover stood at 2.919 billion shares worth RM3.214 billion.

Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd emerged as the biggest loser, tumbling RM1.38 to RM36.62, while plantation heavyweight United Plantations Bhd and consumer stock Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd also posted declines at 26 sen and 22 sen respectively.

Banking and industrial counters also remained under pressure, with Hong Leong Bank Bhd and Hong Leong Industries Bhd among the notable laggards.

On the upside, Nestlé (M) Bhd led the charge, rising RM5.50 to RM108.40, followed by Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd, which climbed 66 sen to RM21.22. Other notable gainers included LPI Capital Bhd, Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd and MISC Bhd.

The market’s weak finish underscored continued caution among investors, as profit-taking and sector rotation shaped trading momentum heading into the new month.

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates April 30, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on April 30, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: Ringgit Opens Lower Against Dollar, Mixed Against Others
data.description:
The ringgit opened lower against the US dollar and was mixed against other currencies, as support from firmer oil prices was offset by broad dollar strength and cautious positioning following the US Federal Reserve (Fed)’s rate decision, Bernama reported.

At 8.04 am, the domestic unit eased against the greenback to 3.9565/9630, from 3.9495/9540 at Wednesday’s close.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the Fed was inclined to keep rates steady, as economic activity continued to expand at a solid pace despite slower job growth.

At its third meeting of the year, the Federal Open Market Committee maintained the target range for the federal funds rate at 3.50 per cent to 3.75 per cent.

“With the sharp spike in crude oil prices, the ringgit is likely to weaken today, with the US dollar/ringgit pair potentially attempting to breach RM3.96,” Mohd Afzanizam told Bernama.

At the opening, the ringgit was traded mixed against a basket of major currencies, and against its ASEAN peers.

It rose slightly against the Japanese yen to 2.4700/4744 from 2.4709/4739 on Wednesday, but weakened against the British pound to 5.3381/3469 from 5.3330/3391 and slipped against the euro to 4.6236/6312 from 4.6197/6250.

It edged down against the Singapore dollar to 3.0905/0659 from 3.0909/0946 at Wednesday’s close, but strengthened against the Thai baht to 12.0765/1022 from 12.0791/0980.

The ringgit eased against the Indonesian rupiah to 228.3/228.8 from 227.9/228.3 and fell against the Philippine peso to 6.42/6.44 from 6.41/6.42.

data.title: Profit-Taking Pulls Bursa Lower At Midday As Fed Caution Weighs On Sentiment
data.description:
The FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) slipped into negative territory at the midday break, as investors locked in profits after recent market gains, while cautious sentiment lingered following the US Federal Reserve’s (Fed) decision to hold interest rates steady.

At 12:30 pm, the benchmark index eased 4.23 points, or 0.24%, to 1,716.19 from its close of 1,720.42 on April 29, tracking softer regional markets as investors digested Fed Chair Jerome Powell’s warning that inflation in the US remains stubbornly elevated.

The broader indices also trended lower, with the FBM 70 dropping 141.24 points (0.78%) to 18,056.86 and the FBM Emas declining 53.23 points (0.42%) to 12,689.97, underscoring a cautious trading mood as investors recalibrated expectations amid global macroeconomic uncertainty.

Broader market sentiment was weak, with losers outpacing gainers 747 to 292, reflecting selling pressure across selected utilities, financial services, and technology counters.

Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd topped the losers’ list, shedding 66 sen to RM37.34, alongside declines in United Plantations Bhd and Hong Leong Bank Bhd.

On the upside, Nestlé (M) Bhd emerged as the top gainer, surging RM5.90 to RM108.80, followed by LPI Capital Bhd and Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd.

Among active counters, Zetrix AI Bhd led the volume chart, falling 5.5 sen to 80 sen, while Borneo Oil Bhd and Econpile Holdings Bhd also saw brisk trading activity.

The overall turnover stood at 1.64 billion units worth RM1.26 billion.

data.title: Singapore Shares Start Strong, STI Up Nearly 1% In Early Trade
data.description:
Singapore shares opened higher, with the Straits Times Index (STI) rising 0.96% to 4,907.82 in early trade as investors tracked a mixed Wall Street close and ongoing global market uncertainty.

Market breadth was slightly positive at the open, with 108 gainers against 106 decliners. Total early turnover stood at 153.63 million shares worth S$302.62 million.

Sentiment was supported by a steadier tone in global equities after US markets ended mixed, as investors weighed the US Federal Reserve’s decision to hold interest rates steady and concerns over rising oil prices amid geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

Energy-linked volatility remained in focus after crude prices climbed on supply disruption fears linked to potential restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz.

On Wall Street, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.57%, while the S&P 500 edged down 0.04% and the Nasdaq Composite inched up 0.04%, reflecting a cautious risk environment ahead of major technology earnings.

Regional derivatives trade was active, with MSCI Singapore Index Futures for May 2026 last at 438.00.

data.title: Bitcoin Steady At US$78,000 As Ethereum Consolidates
data.description:
Major cryptocurrencies opened May on a steady footing, with Bitcoin hovering near the US$78,000 mark while Ethereum traded around US$2,300, reflecting a cautious but resilient market tone.

Bitcoin’s recent rebound has kept it above key support levels, with traders increasingly expecting the token to remain above US$76,000 in the near term despite limited upside momentum. Market pricing suggests a range-bound trajectory rather than a decisive breakout, as investors weigh macroeconomic uncertainty and shifting risk sentiment.

Ethereum, meanwhile, has entered a consolidation phase, with most expectations centred on the US$2,200-US$2,300 band for May 1 trading. The muted movement signals steady institutional support, though conviction remains thin amid weak retail participation.

The broader crypto market remains highly sensitive to external factors, including interest-rate expectations, technology sector performance and geopolitical tensions.

While both assets have recovered from early-2026 lows, the current rally lacks the widespread enthusiasm seen in previous bull cycles, suggesting investors are adopting a more measured stance.

For now, the market appears supported, but still fragile, as May begins with consolidation rather than acceleration.

data.title: Hang Seng Futures Rally Faces Key Resistance At 26,600
data.description:
Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) extended its counter-trend rebound, closing 233 points higher at 25,940, according to RHB Investment Bank (RHB Research).

The index opened at 25,549 and dipped to a low of 25,439 before gaining momentum to hit an intraday high of 26,009, supported by renewed bullish sentiment.

RHB said the HSIF has now broken above both its 20-day and 50-day simple moving averages, with the shorter-term average crossing above the longer-term line, signalling near-term strength.

Despite the rebound, the research house maintained a bearish trading stance, noting that resistance at 26,600 remains a key ceiling. The index is expected to face selling pressure and potentially retrace towards the 25,100 support level.

Traders are advised to maintain short positions initiated at 26,367, with a stop-loss set at 26,600 to manage downside risks.

Immediate support is seen at 25,100, followed by 24,500, while resistance levels are pegged at 26,600 and 27,200.

data.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq Hit Record Highs As Earnings Lift Wall Street
data.description:
Wall Street ended the week on a strong note, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite closing at fresh record highs on May 1, powered by robust corporate earnings and easing crude prices.

Reuters reported that the S&P 500 rose 21.11 points, or 0.29%, to 7,230.12, while the Nasdaq climbed 222.13 points, or 0.89%, to 25,114.44. The Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 152.87 points, or 0.31%, to 49,499.27.

Both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq notched their sixth straight week of gains, their longest winning streak since October 2024, as investors digested one of the strongest earnings seasons in recent years.

Analysts now expect first-quarter earnings growth of 27.8% year-on-year, marking the biggest expansion since late 2021. Of the 314 companies that have reported so far, 83% have beaten profit estimates.

Technology shares led gains, with Apple rising 3.3% after issuing a strong sales forecast. Software stocks also rallied, with Atlassian surging 29.6% after lifting its annual outlook.

Energy stocks lagged as oil prices eased following reports that Iran had submitted a fresh proposal for talks with the US, reducing immediate supply concerns.

Despite geopolitical uncertainty and inflation pressures, investor optimism remained anchored by earnings resilience, helping US equities build on their strongest monthly gains in years.

data.title: Decliners Dominate Bursa As 617 Losers Drag Market Lower At Close
data.description:
Decliners led the charge on FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) at the close on April 29, with 617 stocks ending in the red against 485 gainers, reflecting broad-based weakness across the market.

Trading sentiment softened as turnover fell to 3.63 billion units worth RM3.13 billion, down from 3.85 billion units valued at RM3.38 billion in the previous session, signalling reduced investor appetite.

Among the top losers, Hong Leong Industries Bhd dropped 30 sen to RM17.50, while Petronas Dagangan Bhd declined 26 sen to RM20.30. Malayan Banking Bhd also weighed on sentiment, sliding 18 sen to RM11.04.

Other notable decliners included SAM Engineering & Equipment (M) Bhd, down 21 sen to RM3.97, and Petronas Gas Bhd, which shed 14 sen to RM17.82.

On the upside, gains were led by Nestlé (M) Berhad, which rose 60 sen to RM102.90, followed by Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd, up 50 sen to RM38.

Despite selective buying in heavyweight counters, negative breadth and weaker turnover underscored a cautious mood as investors trimmed positions across sectors.

data.title: Meta Shares Fall On Concerns Over AI Spending, Legal Scrutiny
data.description:
Meta Platforms raised its annual capital spending forecast on Wednesday, signaling plans to pour billions more into artificial intelligence infrastructure even as it confronts potential losses from a global youth backlash against social media.

The Facebook and Instagram parent projected 2026 capital expenditure between $125 billion and $145 billion, compared with its prior forecast of $115 billion to $135 billion.

Shares of the company fell more than 6 per cent in extended trading.

Meta also warned that legal and regulatory blowback in the European Union and the U.S. “could significantly impact our business and financial results,” after years of mounting criticism about children’s safety on social media.

“We continue to see scrutiny on youth-related issues and have additional trials scheduled for this year in the U.S., which may ultimately result in a material loss,” it said.

The company is facing a rising number of teen social media bans around the globe, as well as thousands of court cases by individuals, municipalities, states and school districts that accuse it of designing addictive platforms that are harmful to children.

Several high-stakes court cases are due in the coming months, including the second part of a landmark New Mexico trial and a California case expected to test claims central to nearly 2,000 similar lawsuits filed by U.S. school districts.

Adding to the gloom, Meta reported its first-ever quarterly decline in Daily Active People (DAP) since it started using that metric to measure user numbers across its social media platforms. It attributed the decline to internet disruptions in Iran, as well as restrictions on access to WhatsApp in Russia.

Daily active people grew 4 per cent year-over-year in the first quarter to 3.56 billion.

Matt Britzman, an analyst at Hargreaves Lansdown, said Meta’s higher capital spending spooked investors but is likely overblown as it reflects more expensive memory prices rather than changes to Meta’s investment plan.

‘Optimal Size’

The results come weeks after Reuters first reported Meta’s plans for sweeping layoffs, as CEO Mark Zuckerberg attempts to aggressively integrate AI into the company’s workflows and reshape its workforce around the technology.

The company is planning further layoffs in the second half of the year, sources have told Reuters.

Meta is also installing new tracking software on U.S.-based employees’ computers to capture mouse movements, clicks and keystrokes to train its AI models, part of a broad initiative to build AI agents that can perform work tasks autonomously, Reuters reported last week.

Meta Chief Financial Officer Susan Li confirmed the May layoffs on a conference call after reporting the financial results.

“We don’t really know what the optimal size of a company will be in the future,” Li said. “I think there’s a lot of change right now, with AI capabilities advancing rapidly.”

On the same call, Zuckerberg described seeing tiny teams use AI to make products that previously would have taken dozens of people months to finish.

He said he wanted to build “the next evolution of our company around these people,” which meant investing in top-tier infrastructure, increasing rewards for heavy hitters and “streamlining teams so they aren’t bigger than they need to be.”

Meta said its workforce at the end of March was 77,986 people, up 1 per cent from the same period last year but down from 78,865 at the end of December.

Reflecting anticipated savings, it kept the forecast for total 2026 expenses unchanged despite the projected rise in capital expenditure this year.

The company reported first-quarter revenue of $56.31 billion, beating the LSEG-compiled analysts’ average estimate of $55.45 billion.

It expects second-quarter revenue of $58 billion to $61 billion, largely in line with estimates of $59.5 billion.

But those results were eclipsed by faster growth by other tech companies.

“Meta’s results met expectations, but failed to impress investors, especially in the context of much stronger results from Google,” said Gil Luria, managing director of D.A. Davidson.

He said investors were also concerned that Meta’s spending plans rose without a corresponding reduction in operating expenses.

Google parent Alphabet topped Wall Street estimates for quarterly revenue and profit.

Reuters

data.title: Stock Today: IGB REIT Climbs 3.5% As Buying Momentum Builds Ahead Of Close
data.description:
IGB Real Estate Investment Trust (IGB REIT) was trading higher in afternoon activity, supported by stronger quarterly earnings momentum and sustained investor interest in retail REIT exposure, with the market still open.

At 3.46pm, the counter last traded at RM2.93, up RM0.10 or 3.53%, after moving between RM2.84 and RM2.93 during the session. It opened at RM2.84 and briefly tested intraday highs at RM2.93, matching the current last traded level. Volume stood at 9,737 lots of 100 shares, with buying interest at RM2.93 and selling at RM2.94.

The REIT’s performance was underpinned by its latest financial results, which showed a strong rebound in operating metrics for the first quarter ended 31 March 2026. Net property income rose 55.5% year-on-year to RM207 million, driven by the inclusion of The Mall, Mid Valley Southkey acquired in November 2025, alongside improved contributions from Mid Valley Megamall and The Gardens Mall.

Revenue for the quarter increased 52.4% to RM261.3 million from RM171.4 million a year earlier, reflecting portfolio expansion and firmer leasing activity across its core retail assets.

IGB REIT declared a distribution of RM173 million for the quarter, equivalent to four sen per unit, representing a payout ratio of 97.5% of distributable income.

Despite the strong quarterly performance, management has cautioned that Malaysia’s retail sector may remain subdued in 2026 due to softer consumer sentiment and cost pressures. However, it expects support from tourism-related activity and retail expansion ahead of Visit Malaysia 2026.

The REIT said it will continue focusing on tenant mix optimisation, active asset management and cost discipline to support income stability across its portfolio.

data.title: Late Buying Pushes Bursa Higher As Oil Rally Lifts Energy Counters
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia staged a late-session rebound on April 30, with last-minute buying reversing earlier losses and pushing the benchmark index into positive territory as stronger oil prices boosted energy- and chemical-linked stocks.

At the close, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 1.60 points, or 0.09%, to 1,722.02 from its close of 1,720.42 during the previous day trading session.

The turnaround came despite weaker regional sentiment, as investors across Asia reacted to negative cues from Wall Street, elevated oil prices, mixed corporate earnings and a cautious stance from the US Federal Reserve.

Broader market performance remained mixed. The FBM 70 fell 112.13 points (0.62%) to 18,085.97, while the FBM EMAS and F4GBM each slipped 19.46 points (0.15%) and 1.57 points (0.15%) to close at 12,723.74 and 1,027.26, respectively.

Trading activity stayed active, led by Zetrix AI Bhd, which topped the volume chart at 155.3 million shares changing hand despite falling 3.5 sen to 82 sen. Other actively traded counters included UEM Sunrise Bhd, VS Industry Bhd, AirAsia X Bhd and Econpile Holdings Bhd.

The late recovery highlighted selective bargain hunting, with investors rotating into sectors expected to benefit from firmer oil prices while remaining cautious on broader market risks.

data.title: Wall Street Rallies To Multi-Year Monthly Highs As Earnings Trump Oil Shock
data.description:
Wall Street closed sharply higher on April 30, with major US indexes capping their strongest monthly gains in years as upbeat corporate earnings and resilient economic data helped investors look past geopolitical tensions and soaring oil prices.

Reuters reported that the Dow Jones Industrial Average surged 790.33 points, or 1.62%, to 49,652.14, while the S&P 500 gained 1.02% to 7,209.00 and the Nasdaq Composite added 0.89% to 24,892.31.

The strong finish sealed a standout month for equities. The S&P 500 posted its biggest monthly percentage gain since November 2020, while the Nasdaq recorded its strongest monthly advance since April 2020. The Dow marked its best month since November 2024.

Investor sentiment was lifted by robust earnings from major US companies, even as the Middle East conflict continued to fuel concerns over global oil supply disruptions.

Among the tech giants, Alphabet jumped 10% after reporting a record quarter for its cloud business, while Amazon rose 0.8%. However, Meta Platforms and Microsoft fell on concerns over heavy artificial intelligence spending.

Outside tech, Caterpillar climbed 9.9% to a record high after stronger first-quarter profit, helping industrials lead sector gains. Eli Lilly also rallied 9.8% after raising its annual profit forecast.

Economic data added to the bullish tone, showing the US economy expanded 2% in the first quarter of 2026, while jobless claims fell to their lowest level since 1969.

Still, inflation remained above 3%, supported by higher energy prices, reducing expectations for near-term interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve.

Despite the uncertain geopolitical backdrop, advancing stocks decisively outpaced decliners, underscoring strong market breadth as investors closed the month on an optimistic note.

data.title: Japanese Yen Rises Against Dollar On BOJ Intervention
data.description:
The yen suddenly jumped against the dollar on Friday, a day after Tokyo authorities were widely believed to have intervened to prop up the Japanese currency.

The dollar fell by as much as 0.66 per cent to a session low of 155.60 from 157.12 earlier.

The yen rose by as much as 3 per cent on Thursday after an apparent steady stream of official buying pushed the dollar to a low of 155.5 yen from around 158.3 over the course of about an hour, which Reuters and others reported was intervention by Japanese officials.

It was not clear what was behind Friday’s move, but analysts said after Thursday the market would be on edge for any sudden moves in the currency.Japan’s top currency diplomat Atsushi Mimura said earlier on Friday that speculation remained rife, a blunt warning that officials were ready to step back into markets after intervening just hours earlier to support the yen, which has lost 5 per cent in the last three months alone.The Ministry of Finance was not immediately available for comment.

“Liquidity is thin and people are nervous after yesterday so there is a susceptibility to volatility in the dollar/yen,” said Jeremy Stretch, head of G10 FX strategy, CIBC Capital Markets.

“Every time we see a substantial move in the yen there will be questioning about what is driving this given the warnings we have had.”

The wide gap between U.S. and Japanese interest rates, together with an expected drop in trading volumes ahead of a holiday stretch have made officials wary of aggressive speculative attacks.

Reuters

data.title: Asian Share Markets Rebound As Oil Prices Drop And Upbeat Earnings
data.description:
Asian share markets rebounded in relief on Friday as oil prices came off the boil and upbeat company earnings pulled investors into tech stocks, while Japan’s first yen-buying intervention in two years steadied the battered currency.

Apple amplified the cheer by beating forecasts and providing an upbeat outlook for sales, though it did warn of chip supply constraints. Its shares rose 2.7 per cent in extended trading, adding to gains of 10 per cent in both Caterpillar and Alphabet as they beat expectations.

Hopes for ever-rising profits saw the S&P 500 climb more than 10 per cent for all of April, while Nasdaq surged 15 per cent in its best performance since 2020. S&P 500 futures were up 0.2 per cent on Friday, with Nasdaq futures firming 0.1 per cent.

April was also a barnstormer for Asia, with Japan’s Nikkei up 16 per cent for the month, Taiwan gaining 23 per cent and South Korea almost 31 per cent.Market holidays limited the reaction across Asia on Friday, with the Nikkei up 0.4 per cent and Australian shares adding 0.7 per cent. MSCI’s broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan edged 0.3 per cent higher.

Asia does remain acutely vulnerable to higher energy prices, importing most of its oil and gas, and oil flows remain badly disrupted through the vital Strait of Hormuz.

Iran said on Thursday it would respond with “long and painful strikes” on U.S. positions if Washington renewed attacks and restated its claim to the strait.

That saw Brent crude firm 1.2 per cent to $111.70 a barrel, though that was well off Thursday’s four-year peak of $126.41. U.S. crude rose 0.5 per cent to $105.64 a barrel.

JAPAN DRAWS A LINE FOR YEN

Currency markets had also come alive after sources said Japanese authorities had intervened on Thursday to sell dollars for yen, initially sending the greenback sliding five whole yen to a two-month low of 155.50.

Yet buyers were back on Friday, lifting the dollar to 157.29 in a sign Tokyo may yet have to do more if it really wants to draw a line at the 160.00 yen barrier.

“The cost is likely to be in the tens of billions of dollars based on history,” said Tim Baker, a macro strategist at Deutsche Bank, referring to the size of the intervention.

“We’re not convinced USD/JPY will keep falling, or even stay here for long,” he argued. “The cross may well be high relative to rates, but it’s actually low relative to a simple model that includes rates, equities and oil.”

Japan imports all its oil and the rise in crude prices is set to sharply widen the country’s trade deficit.

The burst of dollar sales indirectly lifted the euro to $1.1729 and away from a three-week trough of $1.1655. The pound firmed as far as a 10-week high at $1.3612.

Both currencies were supported by hawkish commentary from their respective central banks.

The Bank of England warned that the fallout from the Iran war could lead to “forceful” rate rises if energy prices kept climbing, and one board member voted for an immediate hike.

European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said they were debating whether to lift rates and noted that data over the next six weeks would decide the issue.

“The messages conveyed during the press conference leave us with a distinct perception that the consensus among governors is that they will hike policy rates at the next meeting on June 11,” said analysts at Citi in a note.

“We find no reason to alter our expectation of back-to-back rate hikes in June and July.”

That follows a hawkish shift from the Federal Reserve on Wednesday that saw markets give up on any hope for a rate cut there this year.

The pivot left U.S. 10-year Treasury yields up 8 basis points on the week at 4.390 per cent, but off a top of 4.436 per cent.

Elsewhere in commodity markets, gold was flat at $4,623 an ounce, having been stuck in a tight trading range for more than a month now. 

Reuters

data.title: Wall Street Ends Mixed As Oil Surge, Fed Decision Keep Markets On Edge
data.description:
Wall Street ended mixed as investors weighed rising oil prices, the Federal Reserve’s interest rate decision and a wave of major technology earnings released after the close.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.57% to 48,861.81, while the S&P 500 slipped 0.04% to 7,135.98. The Nasdaq Composite edged up 0.04% to 24,673.24.

Markets fluctuated after the Federal Reserve held interest rates steady, with policymakers showing their most divided stance since 1992 amid uncertainty over inflation driven by higher energy prices.

Crude prices climbed after the White House confirmed that Donald Trump had instructed officials to prepare for a prolonged blockade of Iranian ports, raising concerns over continued supply disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

Investor focus also turned to earnings from major US technology firms, including Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon and Meta Platforms, which reported results after the closing bell. In extended trading, Alphabet shares rose more than 3%, while Amazon and Microsoft fell over 3%, and Meta declined more than 6%.

Energy stocks led gains within the S&P 500, supported by higher oil prices, while utilities and materials sectors posted the steepest losses.

Among individual stocks, Robinhood Markets dropped 13.2% after missing profit expectations, while Starbucks jumped 8.5% after raising its annual forecast. Visa gained 8.3% on improved earnings guidance.

Data-storage firms rallied following a strong outlook from Seagate Technology, with its shares surging 11.1%.

On the economic front, US core capital goods orders rose 3.3% in March, marking the largest increase since June 2020, signalling resilience in corporate spending despite global uncertainties.

Trading volume on US exchanges totalled 16.37 billion shares, below the 20-day average of 17.81 billion.

Reuters

data.title: Weak Start For Bursa As Profit-Taking Drags Key Benchmarks
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened lower on Thursday, with all major indices trading in negative territory as investors tracked a mixed session on Wall Street and rising global oil price concerns.

At 9.30am, the FBM KLCI slipped 5.62 points or 0.33% to 1,714.80. The FBM70 dropped 0.59% to 18,091.14, while the FBMEMAS eased 0.42% to 12,690.10. The FBMSHA declined 0.53% to 12,640.38, and the F4GBM index fell 0.44% to 1,024.26.

The softer start mirrored Wall Street’s overnight performance, where the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.57%, while the S&P 500 slipped 0.04% and the Nasdaq Composite edged up 0.04% in a mixed close.

Global sentiment remained cautious as markets weighed higher oil prices driven by geopolitical tensions, alongside the Federal Reserve’s decision to hold interest rates steady amid its most divided policy stance in decades.

Energy concerns linked to potential supply disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz continued to influence investor appetite, while US earnings from major technology companies added further volatility to global risk sentiment.

data.title: Stock Today: Hibiscus Slips As Market Digests Shareholder Stake Reduction
data.description:
Hibiscus Petroleum Bhd shares were trading slightly weaker in afternoon trade, as the counter reacted to fresh disclosures involving a substantial shareholder’s indirect stake reduction, though overall movement remained relatively contained with the market still open.

At 3.09pm, the stock was last done at RM2.20, down 0.45% or RM0.01. It opened at RM2.25 and touched an intraday high of RM2.25 before easing to a low of RM2.19, suggesting mild selling pressure after early-session firmness. Volume stood at 124,776 lots of 100 shares, with buy and sell queues at RM2.20 and RM2.21 respectively.

Sentiment was partly influenced by Bursa filings showing that Datuk Michael Tang Vee Mun, a substantial indirect shareholder, disposed of a combined 886,300 shares over two consecutive days. The transactions comprised 500,000 shares on 27 April 2026 and 386,300 shares on 28 April 2026, carried out via CGS International Nominees Malaysia (Asing) Sdn Bhd under a pledged securities account for Polo Investments Ltd.

Following the disposals, his indirect deemed interest stands at 82,258,226 shares, or 11.155% of Hibiscus Petroleum’s issued share capital.

While the shareholding update introduced some caution among investors, price action so far suggests a measured reaction rather than a broad-based sell-off, with the counter still moving within a tight intraday range as trading continues.
